title: Applying Multi-Document YAML
description:
## Applying Multi-Document YAML

A single YAML file can contain **multiple Kubernetes resources** separated by `---`. This is useful for packaging related resources (e.g., a Deployment and its Service) into one file for atomic application.

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: first
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: second
```

**Your task:**

Create a single file `/tmp/app-stack.yaml` containing **three resources** separated by `---`:
1. A **Namespace** named `multi-doc-ns`
2. A **ConfigMap** named `stack-config` in `multi-doc-ns` with key `version=v1`
3. A **Deployment** named `stack-app` in `multi-doc-ns` using image `nginx:alpine` with 1 replica

Apply all three resources with a single `kubectl apply -f /tmp/app-stack.yaml` command.

```bash
# Apply all at once:
kubectl apply -f /tmp/app-stack.yaml

# Verify all three were created:
kubectl get namespace multi-doc-ns
kubectl get configmap stack-config -n multi-doc-ns
kubectl get deployment stack-app -n multi-doc-ns
```

hints.title: Write the multi-document YAML
hints.body: Use a heredoc to write all three resources with --- separators into a single file.
hints.command:
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/app-stack.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: multi-doc-ns
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: stack-config
  namespace: multi-doc-ns
data:
  version: v1
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: stack-app
  namespace: multi-doc-ns
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: stack-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: stack-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:alpine
EOF

hints.title: Apply the file
hints.body: One kubectl apply command processes all documents in the file.

commands.command: kubectl get configmap stack-config -n multi-doc-ns -o jsonpath='{.data.version}'
kubectl get deployment stack-app -n multi-doc-ns -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
